message:
🌱 Add k3s: Support for dual-stack Kubernetes cluster mission (#934)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,81 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "k3s-1405-support-for-dual-stack-kubernetes-cluster",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "k3s: Support for dual-stack Kubernetes cluster",
+    "description": "Support for dual-stack Kubernetes cluster. Requested by 36+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current k3s deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your k3s version and configuration:\n```bash\\nk3s --version\\nkubectl get nodes\\n```\\nThis feature requires a working k3s installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review k3s configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant k3s configuration:\n```bash\\nk3s --version\\nkubectl get nodes -o wide\\nkubectl get pods -A\\n```\nCurrently, the parameter inputs (--cluster-cidr, --serice-cidr, etc) do not support more than one entry and that entry must be an IPv4 range."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support for dual-stack Kubernetes cluster",
+        "description": "Problem: Although Kubernetes does support dual stack, K3S does not\n\nSolution: Modify K3S so it can both support parameters for dual stack,\nand pass them to the different Kubernetes daemons\n\n#### Proposed Changes ####\n\nAdd dual stack support to K3S\n\n#### Types of Changes ####\n\nNew Feature\n\n####\n```yaml\nk3s server \\\n        '--tls-san' \\\n        '{{ k3s_master }}' \\\n        '--no-deploy' \\\n        'servicelb' \\\n        '--no-deploy' \\\n        'traefik' \\\n        '--docker' \\\n        '--flannel-backend=none' \\\n        '--default-local-storage-path=/dev/shm' \\\n        '--cluster-cidr=10.244.0.0/16,<RANGE>:c101:5810::/64' \\\n        '--service-cidr=10.43.0.0/16,<RANGE>:c101:5811::/64' \\\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n k3s -l app.kubernetes.io/name=k3s\nkubectl get events -n k3s --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Support for dual-stack Kubernetes cluster\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Problem: Although Kubernetes does support dual stack, K3S does not\n\nSolution: Modify K3S so it can both support parameters for dual stack,\nand pass them to the different Kubernetes daemons\n\n#### Proposed Changes ####\n\nAdd dual stack support to K3S\n\n#### Types of Changes ####\n\nNew Feature\n\n#### Verification ####\nYou'll need to start K3S with the `DualStack` feature flag.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "k3s server \\\n        '--tls-san' \\\n        '{{ k3s_master }}' \\\n        '--no-deploy' \\\n        'servicelb' \\\n        '--no-deploy' \\\n        'traefik' \\\n        '--docker' \\\n        '--flannel-backend=none' \\\n        '--default-local-storage-path=/dev/shm' \\\n        '--cluster-cidr=10.244.0.0/16,<RANGE>:c101:5810::/64' \\\n        '--service-cidr=10.43.0.0/16,<RANGE>:c101:5811::/64' \\",
+        "/usr/local/bin/k3s server --disable traefik –-flannel-backend=none --disable-network-policy  --disable servicelb --no-flannel --disable-kube-proxy  \\\n\t\t--node-ip <node IPv4 IP> \\\n\t\t--node-ip <node IPv6 IP> \\\n\t\t--kube-apiserver-arg 'feature-gates=IPv6DualStack=true' \\\n\t\t--kube-controller-manager-arg 'feature-gates=IPv6DualStack=true' \\\n\t\t--kubelet-arg 'feature-gates=IPv6DualStack=true' \\\n\t\t--cluster-cidr '<cluster IPv4 cidr>,<cluster IPv6 cidr>' \\\n\t\t--service-cidr '<service IPv4 cidr>,<cluster IPv6 cidr>' \\",
+        "# services \"kube-dns\" was not valid:\n# * spec.ipFamilyPolicy: Invalid value: \"RequireDualStack\": Cluster is not configured for dual stack services"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "k3s",
+      "sandbox",
+      "orchestration",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "k3s"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Node",
+      "Service"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/k3s-io/k3s/issues/1405",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/k3s-io/k3s",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/k3s-io/k3s/pull/3049"
+    },
+    "reactions": 36,
+    "comments": 23,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "k3s",
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running k3s cluster or a system where k3s can be installed."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-13T06:36:59.926Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
